hints.to say: 'to say' is 'dicere' — do not confuse with 'dare'
hints.to go: 'to go' is 'ire' — 'da' is the imperative of 'dare'

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative 1st singular would be 'do' — 'da' is the imperative singular
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'dem' or 'des' — 'da' is imperative
hints.Future imperative: Future imperative would be 'dato' — 'da' is present imperative

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead) — 'da' comes from 'dare'
hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the parts of 'dicere' (to say) — not related to 'dare'
hints.doceo, docere, docui, doctum: These are the parts of 'docere' (to teach) — a different verb

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'castitas' — the -atem ending is accusative singular
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'castitate' — 'castitatem' with -em is accusative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'castitas, castitatis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'castitas' with -tas suffix is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'castitas' is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'continentia' — the -am ending is accusative singular
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'continentiae' — 'continentiam' with -am is accusative

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'continentia' with -ia ending is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension accusatives end in -em — 'continentiam' with -am is 1st declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'continentia' is 1st declension

hints.to not know: 'to not know' is 'nescire' or 'ignorare' — 'noli' comes from 'nolle'
hints.to harm: 'to harm' is 'nocere' — do not confuse with 'nolle'
hints.to wish: 'to wish' is 'velle' — 'nolle' is its negative counterpart (non + velle)

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'nolo' — 'noli' is the imperative form
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'nolim' — 'noli' is imperative
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'nolui' — 'noli' is present imperative

hints.volo, velle, volui: These are the parts of 'velle' (to wish) — 'nolle' is its negative (non + velle)
hints.noceo, nocere, nocui, nocitum: These are the parts of 'nocere' (to harm) — a different verb
hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are the parts of 'noscere' (to come to know) — not related to 'nolle'

steps.prompt: What is the humorous irony of this prayer?
steps.target: Augustine asks God for virtue but begs Him to delay granting it
choices: Augustine asks God for virtue but begs Him to delay granting it
Augustine is quoting someone else's prayer

hints.Augustine rejects chastity entirely: He does ask for chastity — the irony is in 'sed noli modo' (but not yet)
hints.Augustine is quoting someone else's pray: Augustine is recounting his own earlier prayer in his Confessions
hints.Augustine is testing God's patience: The humor is self-directed — Augustine confesses his own weakness, not defiance

translation: Give me chastity and self-control, but not yet.
